task_completion.visual_evidence: The SciNote dashboard or interface is visible in the web browser, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the 'Legacy Archive' project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Legacy Archive' project page is open and active in the SciNote interface.

task_completion.description: Agent created a task named with the format '[Date] - [Experiment]'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A task exists within the 'Legacy Archive' project with a title that clearly contains a date and an experiment name (e.g., 'Oct 12 1998 - Titration' or similar format derived from the scan).

task_completion.description: Agent transcribed the 'Observations' from the image into the task's description or a comment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Transcribed text representing the handwritten/scanned observations is visible in the description field or the comments section of the newly created task.

task_completion.description: Agent uploaded 'notebook_scan.jpg' as an attachment to the task.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'notebook_scan.jpg' is visible in the attachments, files, or protocol section of the newly created task in SciNote.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to interact with SciNote.
integrity.visual_evidence: SciNote is open in a web browser (like Firefox or Chromium), not being manipulated solely via terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Agent entered the transcription directly into the SciNote application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The transcribed text is seen inside the SciNote web interface text boxes, rather than just in a local text editor.

integrity.description: Agent uploaded the actual file from the Desktop.
integrity.visual_evidence: A file upload dialog or drag-and-drop action shows the file being sourced from /home/ga/Desktop/notebook_scan.jpg.
